mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate and supports equity valuations, so declining FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign relationship reverses for more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is still elevated and the policy rate stays above 4.5%, restrictive policy tends to compress multiples and weaken risk appetite, so FEDFUNDS above threshold predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and slower earnings growth, so UNRATE increasing by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX produces positive 20-day returns in a majority of UNRATE-threshold episodes or if the thresholded signal has a rolling Rank IC above 0.03 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling payroll growth and rising unemployment identifies a labor-market slowdown that is more informative than either series alone, and this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample classification of 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in retail sales reflects stronger consumer spending and corporate revenue momentum, so increasing RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month increases in RSAFS fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weaker interest-sensitive demand and a broader growth slowdown, so HOUST falling by more than 5% month over month predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if large negative HOUST shocks are not followed by weaker 60-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is not above 55% after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and GDP both accelerate, real activity is broad-based and earnings expectations improve, so simultaneous increases in INDPRO and GDPC1 predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in INDPRO and GDPC1 does not improve 60-day SPX return forecasts relative to either series alone, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising industrial production indicates firms are restoring output and profit margins, so INDPRO above its recent trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in recovery periods, or if the signal loses sign consistency after controlling for CPIAUCSL changes.
